verses.1: These also are proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out.
verses.2: It is the glory of God to conceal a thing,
verses.3: As the heavens for height, and the earth for depth,
verses.4: Take away the dross from the silver,
verses.5: Take away the wicked from the king’s presence,
verses.6: Don’t exalt yourself in the presence of the king,
verses.7: for it is better that it be said to you, “Come up here,”
verses.8: Don’t be hasty in bringing charges to court.
verses.9: Debate your case with your neighbor,
verses.10: lest one who hears it put you to shame,
verses.12: As an earring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold,
verses.13: As the cold of snow in the time of harvest,
verses.14: As clouds and wind without rain,
verses.15: By patience a ruler is persuaded.
verses.17: Let your foot be seldom in your neighbor’s house,
verses.18: A man who gives false testimony against his neighbor
verses.19: Confidence in someone unfaithful in time of trouble
verses.20: As one who takes away a garment in cold weather,
verses.21: If your enemy is hungry, give him food to eat.
verses.22: for you will heap coals of fire on his head,
verses.24: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop
verses.25: Like cold water to a thirsty soul,
verses.26: Like a muddied spring and a polluted well,
verses.27: It is not good to eat much honey,
verses.28: Like a city that is broken down and without walls
